paper_title: The Fractal Rhythm Model: Mathematical Formalization of Structure and Rhythmicity

phase_ready.note: Retroactive AI layer. Paper published but pending framing review. This paper formalizes the 'fractal' property: self-similar carrier wave architecture across scales. The fractal property is a signature of the law, not a property of any particular substrate.

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:A]
claim_registry.name: Self-similarity across scales
claim_registry.statement: The FRM functional form f(t) = B + A·e^(−λt)·cos(ωt + φ) is self-similar: the same functional form applies at every scale where the FRM conditions are satisfied.

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:D]
claim_registry.name: Fractal property as carrier wave signature
claim_registry.statement: The 'fractal' in Fractal Rhythm Model refers to the self-similar carrier wave architecture across scales. This is a property of the law (the functional form), not of any particular substrate or organization.

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:D]
claim_registry.name: Scale-invariant parameters
claim_registry.statement: The universal constants β = 1/2, k* = π/2, and Γ = 1 + π²/4 are scale-invariant — they take the same values at every scale. Only τ_gen varies with substrate.

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:D]
claim_registry.name: Mathematical structure of FRM
claim_registry.statement: The FRM is the exact transient solution of the Stuart-Landau normal form for μ < 0. The mathematical properties (exponential decay envelope, oscillatory core, phase offset) are derivable from the DDE characteristic equation.

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:F]
claim_registry.name: Scale-invariance of β
claim_registry.statement: β = 1/2 holds across all scales tested in P1 validation set (36 substrates, biological through civilisational). No scale-dependent correction is needed.

falsification_predicate.FALSIFIED_IF: EXISTS scale_class SC IN {biological, physical, social, cognitive, civilisational} SUCH THAT (mean_β(SC) < 0.5 − 3·σ_β(SC)) OR (mean_β(SC) > 0.5 + 3·σ_β(SC))

WHERE.mean_β: scalar · dimensionless · mean β across substrates in scale class SC
WHERE.σ_β: scalar · dimensionless · standard error within scale class

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Compute β per substrate from P3 protocol; group by scale class; test each class against 0.5 ± 3σ; finite
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: 3σ threshold (stricter than P1 F-1.3's 2σ) — testing the stronger claim of exact scale invariance
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: This is the mathematical backbone of the 'fractal' property. If β varies by scale, the self-similarity claim fails.

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:F]
claim_registry.name: Fractal-like behavior from carrier wave architecture
claim_registry.statement: The self-similar appearance across scales (fractal-like behavior) emerges from the carrier wave structure, not from fractal geometry. Removing the oscillatory component (ω → 0) destroys the self-similarity.

WHERE.self_similarity_index_with_oscillation: scalar · dimensionless · cross-scale correlation of FRM fits with full ω
WHERE.self_similarity_index_without_oscillatio: scalar · dimensionless · cross-scale correlation with ω forced to 0 (pure exponential)

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Fit FRM at multiple scales; compute cross-scale correlation; repeat with ω=0; compare; finite
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: [PLACEHOLDER: requires multi-scale dataset with at least 3 scale classes]

placeholder_register.description: Multi-scale self-similarity test. Requires datasets across at least 3 scale classes.
